Sales funnels are an alternative to traditional websites that allow you to…
GENERATE LEADS 	SCHEDULE
APPOINTMENTS
MAKE PRODUCT SALES
Funnels will work on any type of device. You can send people to your sales funnel
from any type of traffic source including paid ads, email marketing, search
engines and social media.
INTRO TO SALES FUNNELS
OPT-IN PAGE
(Collects Email)
SALES PAGE
(Sells Product)
CHECKOUT PAGE
(Collects Card Info)
UPSELL PAGE
(One-Click Sale)
CONFIRMATION PAGE
(Summarizes Order)
Example page path of a classic sales funnel
A funnel is a series of web pages that take your
visitors through a step-by-step process.
Generally speaking, each page is designed to
achieve one specific objective.
We will cover some options for funnel building
and management in this manual as well.
In this manual, we’ll discuss several different
types of sales funnels you can deploy.
HOW DO SALES FUNNELS WORK?
WHAT ARE FUNNELS AND WHAT CAN THEY DO FOR YOU?
A key component to any good sales funnel is the automated follow-up.
The most basic and frequently used follow-up is email autoresponders,
but we will cover additional options in this manual.

While many companies will use both a traditional website as well as a funnel
(or multiple funnels) in most situations a funnel will convert better than a website.
Here’s why...
OFFICIAL RECOMMENDATION
While you can use various systems to build funnels, at 10X we exclusively use
Clickfunnels (10xresource.com/clickfunnels). Clickfunnels allows anyone,
without technical experience, build and deploy funnels that produce great
results. Clickfunnels has had countless success stories. That said, you can
use any funnel builder you wish and we will provide some additional
options in this manual.
What I love about sales funnels is that you don’t need to
be a techie to build and edit them. Keep it simple and
resist the temptation to add bells and whistles.
WHY SEND TRAFFIC TO A FUNNEL INSTEAD OF A WEBSITE?
FUNNELS REDUCES
USER OPTIONS
While websites will
have menus and
many things to
distract a user, every
step of your funnel is
ideally designed for
conversion.
By breaking the sales
process into smaller
pieces, it is easier for
the prospect to
babystep their way to
giving you money.
FUNNELS GET SMALL
COMMITMENTS
Depending on your
traffic source, you can
easily deploy variations
of your funnel that
speak directly to
different audiences.
FUNNELS CAN BE
EASILY CUSTOMIZED

SALES FUNNEL TERMINOLOGY
Let’s review the basic components of a funnel...
Sometimes referred to as a “squeeze page” or “opt-in page”, this is generally the first page
in the process where basic info such as name and email is captured. As with anything in a
funnel, this step is optional and customizable.
OPT-IN FORM
This is where users enter and submit information.
LEAD MAGNET
This is the free giveaway to incentivize the user to provide their info.
SALES PAGE
This is In some situations, you may want to send traffic directly to the sales page
instead of a landing page.
LANDING PAGE
WEB PAGE TERMINOLOGY
5
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 43 --

2-step checkout embedded directly on the sales page. This is a feature exclusive
to the Clickfunnel platform (10xresource.com/clickfunnels)
I like to think of funnels like building blocks and draw
them out on a whiteboard. You can move around the
pages and parts in any way you can imagine.
BUMP OFFER
This is an add-on sale that is a part of the checkout, usually in the form of a checkbox.
UPSELL PAGES
These are add-on sales that come after the initial checkout. Sometimes referred to
as “one-time offers” (or OTOs), most funnel systems will allow you to offer upsells
with one-click.
CALENDAR BOOKING
Not all sales funnels collect money. Some collect and qualify leads for sales
appointments or demos.
CHECKOUT PAGE
This is where the credit card information is entered. Note that some funnels may
have this step on the sales instead of as a standalone page.
6
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 43 --

Here are the money terms that happen behind the scenes...
SUBSCRIPTIONS
Any sort of recurring payment plan which may or may not have a trial period. You may
sometimes hear this referred to as “continuity”.
SHOPPING CART
This simply refers to your funnel’s capability to collect money. Note that most funnels
do not have a classic shopping cart page where you can add or remove items like on
an ecommerce website.
PAYMENT GATEWAY
This is the bridge between your shopping cart and the merchant processor. Many funnel
systems such as 10xresource.com/clickfunnels have options for this built in.
MERCHANT PROCESSOR
This is the company that actually runs the cards with Visa, Mastercard, Amex, etc. Your
merchant provider is who pays you, generally in batches that are released directly to your
bank account a few days later.
Some terms you’ll hear regarding your list…
CRM (CUSTOMER RELATIONS MANAGER)
This is the main control center for your business and where all the customer and
subscriber data is stored. Some funnel building systems can serve as your CRM as well
(such as Clickfunnels) or you can use a different program for your CRM and send funnel
data into it. CRMs come in a wide range of complexities and price ranges, but small
businesses sometimes use a simple email marketer as a CRM.
Customer 	Shopping Cart 	Payment
Gateway 	Merchant Processor 	Your Bank
Account
How money flows from your customer to your bank account.
FINANCIAL TERMINOLOGY
INFORMATION AND DATA

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 43 --

SUBSCRIBER
This is a user that provides their information as well as permission to be marketed to.
At times this term will also refer to a customer who pays for a subscription service.
UNSUBSCRIBE
In the case of email, this is someone who opts out of receiving messages.
BOUNCE RATE
This is the percentage of people who land on your page, but quickly leave. While various
sources define Bounce Rate slightly differently (such as the amount of time on page,
etc) the most important thing is that you get your bounce rate as low as possible.
8

TYPES OF SALES FUNNELS
We are going to lay out a few of the fundamental types of funnels here, but it is important to
remember that no two funnels are ever the same. You can mix and match the content and
sequence of your pages to create the ideal funnel for your objective.
The primary objective of this funnel is not to sell, but rather to create an interest
and get the user to book an appointment. This funnel is used to sell products or
services via phone or video conference.
FUNNEL TYPE #1 -
APPOINTMENT FUNNEL

Product funnels can sell any type of product including physical products, digital
products and subscriptions. Unlike ecommerce websites, product funnels focus on
selling one product at a time, and then often upsell additional products. This is
effective because the initial buying decision is easier and once the payment
information is collected, upsells can be done with one-click.
FUNNEL TYPE #2 -
PRODUCT FUNNEL
After booking an appointment online always be sure to
give the prospect a video to watch before the sales call.
This can make all the difference in closing the deal.
Note that the opt-in page before the sales page is optional

-- 11 of 43 --

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
The sales page of a product funnel can be one of two types of pages
(or a combination of the two)…
Sales letters are traditional sales pages that rely mostly on headlines, sales copy and
images. Sales letters are great for low priced products or subscriptions.
A great sales letter, as with any page, starts with a great headline. Make a bold
promise to either relieve the readers pain or excite them for a unique opportunity.
Intermix both text and images on the page to keep the reader scrolling. Contrary to
popular belief, it is generally a good thing for your sales letters to be longer in length.
Write as much as is needed to make the sale.
These type of pages are primarily focused on a sales video (sometimes referred to as
a video sales letter or “VSL”). Video sales pages great for mid priced products or
subscriptions.
A video sales letter is very similar to a traditional sales letter, but the storyline of the
sale happens in video format instead of in copy on the page (though sometimes the
onpage copy will mimic the video). This ensures a viewer consumes every bit of your
sales message for as long as they continue to watch. To keep the viewer in suspense
on the offer, a common strategy on a VSL page is to have the shopping cart button
“magically” appear after the video makes the call to action.
Most people don’t read every word of a page, they are
skimmers. Make sure that you use headlines, bold text
and highlights to convey your most important points.
You’ve got 10 seconds to capture someone’s attention
in a video. Come out swinging. If you don’t nail the
beginning, the rest of the video is useless.
TYPES OF SALES PAGES
1 	SALES LETTERS
2 	VIDEO SALES PAGE

Webinars are great, especially if you don’t have a
sales team to do the selling for you. Remember on
your webinar that facts tell and stories sell.
Webinars are online presentations which allow you to make a connection with your
prospect and provide a significant amount of information to help sell your offer. This type
of funnel is great for high priced products or services.
FUNNEL TYPE #3 -
WEBINAR FUNNEL
It is important to know that webinars can either be hosted live or recorded and
broadcasted with an automated webinar system like 10XResource.com/Clickfunnels.
Generally speaking, a live sales webinar converts a higher percentage of attendees, but an
auto webinar has obvious advantages. In addition to running 365 days a year, they often
have higher registration rates and attendance rates.

-- 13 of 43 --

HOOKS, OFFERS & LEAD MAGNETS
Now that you have a basic understanding of how funnels work we can
start to discuss what it takes to get results!
At every point within your funnel the user must have very clear incentive to take the desired action. Without
having to scroll down, you want your user to instantly understand the answer to “What’s in it for me?”
For this reason, the headline at the top of the page is often considered the most important aspect of
any funnel page. Your headline should include something that is compelling for the reader.
They call this your “hook” because it should grab their attention enough that they can’t get away.
This will reduce bounce rate and increase conversion rate.
Don’t get in your own head and tell yourself that
writing is hard. Remember that people are motivated
by 2 things: seeking pleasure and avoiding pain.
WRITING COMPELLING HEADLINES
There are many formulas that are used for writing compelling hooks. You can offer to
alleviate the viewer’s pains and fears, or you can do the opposite and get the reader
excited for something that will bring them happiness. You can be very
straightforward with a “how to” headline, the best headlines are ones that can evoke
some sort of emotion from the reader.
You’ll notice that the style and angle of those hooks vary greatly. The most
commonly used emotion in headlines are probably creating curiosity and/or a sense
of urgency. The reader should feel as if they will miss out on something if they don’t
take action, even if that action is to continue reading or watching a video. FOMO
(“fear of missing out”) is certainly nothing new to copywriting.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
EXAMPLES OF CREATIVE HEADLINES:
2 Bizarre Secrets to Getting Rock Hard Abs
Why You Should Punch Your Boss in the Face Tomorrow Morning
Here’s Everything You’ll Miss Out on At the Strike Of Midnight
How to Add 3 Zeros To Your Bank Account Balance This Year
How to Pick Up Chicks (Even if You’re the Ugliest Rooster in the Hen House)
13
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 43 --

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Remember that you’re not just selling a product or service, you’re providing an offer that will
solve a specific problem and/or give a specific benefit. Be sure to check out some of our
funnels for inspiration on how to construct your offers...
This headline is very straightforward as it was used after a full length offer pitch
You might not have a helicopter, but you can get creative!
It is worth noting that straightforward headlines can also be effective (such as the
“prescriptions without hassle” headline above) and humor is always an effective weapon (as
in the “ugliest rooster” above).
In addition to having a great hook, you always need to provide some sort of incentive for
action. In the case of a sales page, this will come in the form of your product or service offer.
When creating your offers you’ve got to really think hard about how to make them extremely
compelling.
Your goal isn’t just to help the reader answer the question 	“Why should I buy this?” 	but
also 	“Why should I buy this RIGHT NOW?” 	This might come in the form of a limited-time
discount, an exclusive bonus bundle or some sort of unique incentive that they can’t get
elsewhere.

Even if something is free doesn’t mean people will be
willing to give you their contact information to get it.
Ask yourself “would I be compelled to opt in for this?”

EXAMPLES OF LEAD MAGNETS:
LEAD MAGNETS
For those pages in your funnels that don’t sell any-
thing, you’ll want to offer some sort of lead magnet to
incentivize your desired action.
While lead magnets are often some sort of digital
download, they don’t have to be. For example, a “sales
call” can be easily reframed as a “free custom strategy
session”. Regardless of the format, the important
thing to remember about your lead magnet is that in
addition to helping generate the lead, they should
also help create an eventual sale.
eBooks and reports
Checklists
Mini-course
Training or webinars
Gift cards or discounts
Starter kit bundles
Free trial
7 day training series
Quizzes
Product demos
Contests
Free ticket

BUILDING YOUR SALES FUNNEL
What is great about funnels is that you don’t need to be technical and you don’t need to
hire people (though you could if you wanted to). This section will give you a good
baseline understanding of how funnel builders work, as well as provide links to
step-by-step tutorials.
NOTE ABOUT EMAIL:
In addition to building funnel pages, you’re also going to want to send
emails. Some funnel systems (such as Clickfunnels) will offer email
features, but you may want to use a separate email marketing platform.
Generally speaking, standalone email marketers can be better for things
such as segmentation and deliverability. They can also be easily
integrated with systems like Clickfunnels. Learn more in the email
marketing manual. (10xdownload.com/email)
Again, we use Clickfunnels (10xresource.com/clickfunnels) for all of our funnels.
For that reason, all examples provided in this manual are from that system.
There are other options which are similar in a lot of ways…
WHAT SYSTEM TO USE
Clickfunnels
Unbounce
Groove Funnels
Lead Pages

Your first step is to choose which type of page you want to build.
Here are the options…
LANDING PAGE OR OPT-IN PAGE
Used for landing page that generate leads but do not sell a product
THANK YOU PAGE
Used as the page after an opt-in page only when you are not selling a product
SALES PAGE
Used to sell a product (note that these pages do not collect the money)
CHECKOUT PAGE
Used to collect money (note that order form pages may be used as sales pages when
you want to checkout form on the page)
Example of page builder with Clickfunnels 2.0

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
ONE-CLICK UPSELL
Used after your order form page to allow a product to be added without requiring the
user to put in information
ONE-CLICK DOWNSELL
Works just like one-click upset pages, but used when users say no to the initial upsell
ORDER CONFIRMATION
This is the page to put at the very end of your funnel that allows the user to see a
summary of what they’ve purchased
APPOINTMENT BOOKING
This page will have a scheduler on it that allows the user to choose an appointment
time, usually for a demo or sales presentation
WEBINAR REGISTRATION
Used specifically for webinars to collect user information and schedule them for a
webinar broadcast

BUILDING PAGES
Remember that a funnel consists of a series of interconnected pages. You’re going to build
one page at a time, and then connect those pages to one another. For now, let’s focus on the
basic elements of a page using a page builder like the one in Clickfunnels.
The first thing you want to understand when building a page is how they are divided and
organized. Think of these as the 3 building blocks of an individual page…
How content is arranged inside the Clickfunnels page editor
HERE IS A VIDEO ABOUT THE CLICK FUNNELS
SECTION/ROW/ELEMENT IMAGE. WE’LL GET IT!

DOWNLOADS AND RESOURCES:
• CF2.0 Adding New Funnel Steps (10xresource.com/cf2sf2)
• CF2.0 Adding Rows (10xresource.com/cf2sf8)
• CF2.0 Adding Elements (10xresource.com/cf2sf9)
• CF2.0 Saving Sections and Rows (10xresource.com/cf2sf14)
• CF2.0 Adding Video Players (10xresource.com/cf2sf10)
SECTIONS
Sections are the largest building blocks of a page. A Section is generally the full width of
the page and will always contain one or more Row. Sections are where you set the page
background which can be either a plain color (often white by default) or add an image
background.
ROWS
Rows are the areas located inside of sections. Each Row may have between 1 to 6 columns
and in these columns you will put the various Elements of your page. You can have
unlimited Rows in each Section.
ELEMENTS
Elements are the various individual features on the page such as headlines, buttons, text,
videos, countdown timers and many more. Elements are added inside of Rows and can So
to recap, a Section contains Rows and Rows contain Elements.
20
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 43 --

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
While Sections and Rows are the structure of the page, Elements are what bring it to life.
Clickfunnels gives you a wide range of Elements that can be easily added. While the type of
Elements available in the editor will vary based on the type of page (for example, the credit card
Element will only appear on order form pages) the majority of Elements are universal.
Systems like Clickfunnels use what is referred to as a WYSIWYG editor (stands for What You
See is What You Get). That simply means that the way you create your pages in the editor is
the same way the user will see it on their devices.
EDITOR AND ELEMENTS
21
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 43 --

Sections, Rows and Elements can all be moved (arrow icon), edited (gear icon), duplicated (paper
icon) or deleted (trash icon) using the simple drag and drop editor. Clicking the plus icon allows you
to add another element.It is also worth noting that you can save Sections or Rows to reuse them on
other pages to save yourself time.
Headline
Sub-Headline
Paragraph (text)
Bullet List
Image
Video Player
Audio Player
Button
Form Elements
Countdown Timers
Icons
Navigation Links
FAQs
Social Share
Progress Bar
CLICKFUNNELS FREQUENTLY USED ELEMENTS
These types of things can take a little bit of time to master,
but the most important thing is that you jump in and
LEARN BY DOING. Watch the tutorials and you’ll get it.

The best upsells will usually fall into one of three
categories: they help the buyer either save money, get
faster results or get more of the results they desire
If you are going to be conducting ecommerce transactions you will need to first set up products
within your funnel builder. In Clickfunnels you will set up your products on each funnel you build.
You can have one-time products or recurring subscription products. You can also offer a free trial
for your subscriptions.
You can also include what is referred to as a “bump offer” on order form pages. This allows the
user to add a product to their order by checking a box. Bumps are great for low-price products
that compliment the original sale. It’s like McDonald’s asking “do you want fries with that?”
Adding bump offers allow you to increase your average cart value
PRODUCTS AND UPSELLS

It is worth noting that all product-based funnels will have one order form page. After the order
form page you can add unlimited upsell pages OR downsell pages (if they say no to an upsell!)
Upsells and downsells pages allow users to add products to the order with just one click,
without needing to re-enter their credit card info.
Creating an upsell or downsell is as simple as adding a funnel step

DOWNLOADS AND RESOURCES:
• CF2.0 Adding Popups (10xresource.com/cf2sf11)
Part of the core functionality of Clickfunnels (and many other funnel builders) is that each
page in the funnel has one popup. The design of popups are completely customizable and
they provide a lot of flexibility functionally as well. Here are a few uses you may find for them:
On the user’s end, a page popup can be be opened by any of the following:
FUNNEL POPUPS
Optin form
Order form
Countdown timers
Bonus notification
Special discount
Give alternate offer
With the click of a button
When user leaves (exit intent)
After a certain amount of time
After scrolling down on page
Common example of opt in form opened by a button click

DOWNLOADS AND RESOURCES:
• CF2.0 Using Mobile Editor (10xresource.com/cf2sf12)
• CF2.0 Hiding Elements (10xresource.com/cf2sf13)
For example, you can create one Row that will look good on desktop and one that will look
good on mobile. Then simply hide the desktop version on mobile and visa versa.
You can learn more about it in Clickfunnels
Roughly half of your visitors will use a mobile device, so you’ll want to make sure your pages
are optimized for both desktop and mobile. Systems like Clickfunnels make it relatively easy
to optimize for each by allowing you to hide Sections, Rows or Elements for either mobile
or desktop.
MOBILE RESPONSIVENESS
26
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 27 of 43 --

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
All funnel builders like Clickfunnels allow you to utilize pre-built templates that can make
building pages faster and easier. If you are new, it is recommended that you start with a
template and then customize it how you want.
Clickfunnels comes with a number of free templates upon purchase that are pre-installed,
but they also have a marketplace where you can purchase premium templates
(10xresource.com/cf2sf20)). If you purchased the 10X Lead System then you have been
provided our pre-built marketing agency templates.
USING TEMPLATES
It is important that you take the shortest path to making
money. Get your funnels working and making sales, then
put time and energy into making them better.
27
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 28 of 43 --

COLLECTING MONEY
While funnel builders like Clickfunnels will serve as your shopping cart, before you begin
selling you will need to set up a payment gateway and a merchant account. While larger
companies generally run more than one merchant account on their payment gateway, when
you’re starting out it is recommended that you use a provider that combines both your
gateway and merchant account together in one.
DOWNLOADS AND RESOURCES:
• CF2.0 Domain Setup (10xresource.com/cf2sf3)
• CF2.0 Path (Page) Names (10xresource.com/cf2sf4)
Systems like Clickfunnels will always provide a default domain name (url) so that you can
immediately create and launch pages. That said, it is highly recommended that you use your
own domain instead. This is much more professional and is better for search engines as well.
You can purchase your domain on Godaddy or any other registrant that you want. After you
do, you can configure it on Clickfunnels by using their provided instructions (see below).
While you will have one domain for all pages on your funnel, each page will have a unique
“page path” This is the information that comes after the domain name. For example,
domainname.com/pagepath.
DOMAINS AND PAGE PATHS
Always click the step settings to name the path for each of your pages
28
https://www.domain.com
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 29 of 43 --

For many online merchants the easiest way to get up and running
with major credit cards is to utilize Stripe. Stripe can be simpler than
alternative solutions both because it integrates with many funnel
systems (such as Clickfunnels) and also because it serves as both the
payment gateway and merchant processor.
If you choose to offer Paypal as an option for payments, realize that
it is unique from typical credit card processing. While Paypal does
allow for the collection of credit cards, it also allows people to pay
with their Paypal account. It can be used as a substitute for
traditional credit card processing or in addition. When orders are
processed through Paypal then Paypal serves as the shopping cart,
the gateway and the processor.
While the advantage of Clickfunnels is that it allows anyone to build a funnel without knowing
code, it does still allow for custom code to be used. You can do so in the following 2 ways:
ADVANCED CUSTOMIZATIONS
1. Custom JS/HTML Element
This allows you to do
things such as add
external widgets or various
items not offered by
Clickfunnels. Examples
include calendar
scheduler, social media
elements or web forms
from external CRMs. These
customizations can often
be done without need of a
professional by following
instructions provided by
the specific tool. 	Easily add scheduling widgets such as this
one from Calendly.
29
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 30 of 43 --

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
2. Custom CSS Settings
This allows the underlying code of the page to be edited in order to change
the layout or design. These customizations cannot be done without coding,
and therefore it is recommended you hire a professional.
It is recommended that before you start building pages that you make a plan for what
pages will be included and how users will flow from step to step. In this manual we covered
4 general types of funnels, but again, they can be combined and rearranged however you see fit.
Draw out the flow on a piece of paper or whiteboard so you can see your customer’s journey.
This will help you understand what to put on each page and give you the needed clarity to bring
your funnel to life.
After your funnel is finished it is very important that you test it before sharing with the world.
Make sure that the lead information goes to the correct place and that sales are being
processed. It is recommended that you test on both desktop and mobile devices to be safe.
Always start by doing the math of your funnel first.
Think about the conversion at each step and how
changing factors such as price will affect overall results.
PLANNING AND TESTING
30
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 31 of 43 --

It is important to remember that all integrations are optional. You will not likely need to use
most of them. Also keep in mind that funnel systems like Clickfunnels will often offer a lot of
optional features such as email and webinars, so it is not necessary to use integrations at all.
Don’t get bogged down in technical stuff until you’ve
proven your concept. If it doesn’t make money without
the bells and whistles, it won’t make money with them.
At 10X we’ve come to utilize a number of tools with our funnels over the years.
Here is a list of some of the most notable ones...
� Hubspot (CRM and Email)
� EZ Texting (Text Marketer)
� Kajabi (Membership Portal)
� Calendly (Appointment Scheduling)
� Google Sheets (Data Storage)
� Slack (Team Communication)
� Zendesk (Support Ticket System)
� ShipEasy (Product Shipping)
� Proof (Social Proof App)
� Everwebinar (Automated Webinars)
� PandaDoc (Legal Contracts)
� Manychat (Messenger Bot)
INTEGRATIONS AND AUTOMATION
There are a wide range of online tools that you can integrate with your funnel. Most funnel
systems, including Clickfunnels, allow you to connect most tools using a 3rd party connector
called Zapier (10xresource.com/zapier)
Think of Zapier as a pipeline that passes data from one web
application to another. With over 1500 different tools, Zapier works
with all major funnel builders, email marketers and virtually every
modern web tool. The major benefit of Zapier is that you do not
need to know code or be technical to use it. Generally speaking, any
non-techie can figure out by watching free tutorials online.
FUNNEL INTEGRATIONS
EXAMPLES INTEGRATIONS
31
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 32 of 43 --

Text Automation
The next type of automated messages to add would likely be text messaging. Text
messages are highly effective, but it is important that you only text people who are
expecting to be texted. We use EZ Texting, but you can use a number of services
for text.
Creating Sequences
After someone opts-in you should create a sequence of messages that guides your
prospect to purchase or achieve the desired action. Generally messages will be
separated by delay timers. Delays between messages can be minutes, hours or
days. They can also be sent on a particular date. When creating sequences, take
into consideration all forms of communication to make a seamless user experience.
Any time you generate a lead or make a sale with your funnel you should ALWAYS have
automated messaging set up to communicate with your contact.
Email Automation
The most common and basic form of automated messages is definitely email. The first
decision you’ll need to make is what system you will use to send your emails. We use
Hubspot (10xresource.com/hubspot) which is a slightly more advanced system. You
may decide to either utilize the email marketing of your funnel system, or to use a more
basic and inexpensive email system such as MailChimp (10xresource.com/mailchimp)
See a full list of Clickfunnel email integrations here (10xresource.com/cf2sf21)
MARKETING AUTOMATION

Message

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.

-- 33 of 43 --

33
Don’t be afraid to send multiple automated messages
about an offer. You need to assume that your subscriber
missed it the first time, because most do.
It should be noted that some systems will have email marketing and/or text marketing built
into them. For example, we use Everwebinar for our automated webinars. Their system has
both text and email that send out before and after the webinar event. Depending on what
tools you use, you may be able to build your autoresponder sequences in a few different ways.
NOTE ON COMPLIANCE:
You should always protect your users data and abide by the rules when
sending messages (such as the Can-Spam act for email). The good news
is that most marketing systems are built to always include opt-out links
in the footer. Additionally, if you do business in the European Union you
will need to add steps to stay compliant with the GDPR.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 34 of 43 --

People stress way too much about traffic. Instead, you
should stress about optimizing your funnels. When you
convert, buying the traffic becomes easy.
You should think about every step of your funnel as a lever that can be pulled to increase your
overall results. Each page will have one primary objective such as...
There are a wide number of things that can be improved on any given sales funnel. While the
importance of each is relative to the specific funnel, here is a list which has been put in a
rough order of relative importance…
OPTIMIZATION FACTORS
Increase opt-in 	Increase sales rate 	Increase cart value
� Overall objective (goal)
� Offer and/or pricing
� Functionality of page
� Hook and/or headlines
� Using/optimizing video
� Using/optimizing a popup
� Adding email/text follow-up
� Overall design of page
� Adding countdown timers
� Adding/removing elements
� Live chat support widget
� Social proof widgets
Example of a social proof widget
34
OPTIMIZING PERFORMANCE
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 35 of 43 --

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
While you will always be optimizing your funnel, it is
important to not try to be perfect. Perfectionists never
get anything done. You can always make it better later.
Always focus on optimizing the pages on the front of the
funnel first. That is where the largest number of people
are and your later pages are useless without it.
While there are some standard “best practices”, there is ultimately only one way to know what
will truly work best in the marketplace... split testing!
Funnel systems such as Clickfunnels make split testing extremely easy. The most common
type of split test is an A/B test, which simply puts 2 pages against one another to see which
performs better. The test will always have a specific goal such as opt-ins or sales. If you don’t
use Clickfunnels, you may need to use a 3rd party platform such as Google Optimize to
conduct split tests.
SPLIT TESTING
Clickfunnels 2.0 allows you to split test and see the data below each page
35
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 36 of 43 --

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Generally speaking, the easier you make things on the user, the higher the conversion will
be. The only situation that does not follow this rule is when you add an extra step to get a
“micro-commitment”. For example, you can ask a simple question before an opt-in form.
This can be effective because it gives the user an easy way to get the process started.
While split testing is important, it should also be noted that you should not split test too
many things at once. First reason is that it can make it difficult to know what worked if you
test multiple items. The other reason being is that split testing can require significant
volume to determine a winner. Start with the big things and go from there.
Question that Clickfunnels uses to get opt-ins on their own website
36
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 37 of 43 --

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Here are a few of the strategies we’ve used to build some of the world’s top converting
funnels. They are all easy to implement and can significantly impact your funnel’s
performance.
The most difficult thing to do in online sales is to get the customer to pull out their
credit card and buy. For that reason, it is often an effective strategy to make the first
offer a very low priced offer that provides a ridiculous amount of value. This is
sometimes referred to as a “Tripwire” or a “Godfather Offer” (because you make them an
offer they can’t refuse!)
This approach makes sense for a number of reasons…
Think of a way that you can create some sort of bundled offer that provides your most
desirable item (even if a smaller version) along with multiple bonus items. Everyone
loves a good deal and if you can craft an offer that gets people excited, it can fuel your
business with leads and sales for years to come
You’ve probably seen a lot of people offer products for
“free” if the buyer just pays shipping. The free + shipping
offer has been around forever because it works.
#1
Once someone
purchases you have
the ability to offer
upsells with one-click
If you can over-deliver
on a small purchase
you can win a
long-term customer
Once someone is a
buyer they are more
likely to buy high-ticket
items in the future
Irresistible Low-Price Offers
37
TIPS, TRICKS AND HACKS
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 38 of 43 --

As mentioned earlier, one of the greatest features of Clickfunnels is the 2-step checkout.
This type of order form works well for 2 reasons…
So while the use and configuration of this form is relatively simple, we will give a few
quick tips…
#2 	Two-Step Checkouts Strategies
You may have heard the term “funnel hacker”. It simply
means to copy what other people are doing. No need to
recreate the wheel, just copy what works.
It starts with a smaller commitment (basic contact info, credit card
info is on step 2) therefore increasing the percentage of people who
begin the process
By collecting email and phone in the first step it allows you to
follow-up with cart abandoners (both via automated emails and
phone calls if you’re able)
For low ticket items, you should
collect as little information as
possible. In other words, if your
merchant account does not
require billing address, skip it.
The shorter the form, the higher
the conversion rate.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
For low ticket items or for sales
that are highly qualified (such as
a webinar) you should put the
2-step checkout at the top of the
page. This will allow the
customer to start the purchase
process quickly and easily.
38
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 39 of 43 --

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
For high ticket items consider hiding the 2 step checkout form in a popup. When
the user clicks on a button, have the popup open containing the 2 step checkout.
We’ve seen this strategy create a big boost.
$15,997
$15,997
Closes soon!
39
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 40 of 43 --

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
There are several types of popups, but the only one we recommend are “exit intent”
popups. Exit intent popups are specially designed so that when a user goes to leave the
page, they are greeted by a nicely designed message before they do. Popups can be
used to offer an alternative product, capture an opt-in or remind the user of a limited
time special.
This type of popup can be created on Clickfunnels (it is one of the options you can
choose in the popup settings) or you can use 3rd party tools such as Optin Monster or
Optimonk. Additionally, many email marketers or CRMs will offer their own free version.
To install one of these popups you usually just need to copy and paste a small piece of
code. Instructions will be provided by each platform.
An important thing to remember about popups is that they are not approved by some
traffic sources. For example, Facebook and Google will not approve ads that send to
pages with popups. That said, if you have traffic from any other source, adding an exit
intent popup will always increase your conversion rates.
#3 	Utilizing Exit Intent Popups
People say things like “I don’t want to add a popup
because they are annoying”. Why worry about
annoying somebody who is leaving your site anyway?
Basic example of an exit intent opt-in popup
